petitioner_background: The record provides no substantive information about the petitioner's background, field of work, or qualifications. The petition was an Immigrant Petition for Alien Worker as an Alien of Extraordinary Ability under Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, filed with the Texas Service Center (SRC 07 111 53013). The petition was denied by the Director, and the matter came before the AAO on appeal.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was rejected as improperly filed. The AAO found that the appeal was not filed by the petitioner or by any entity with legal standing in the proceeding, but rather by the beneficiary through his attorney. Under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(iii)(B), the beneficiary of a visa petition is not considered an 'affected party' with legal standing to appeal. Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(2)(v)(A)(1), an appeal filed by a person or entity not entitled to file it must be rejected as improperly filed, and any filing fee will not be refunded.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, 8 U.S.C. § 1153(b)(1)(A)
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural defect: The appeal was improperly filed by the beneficiary through his attorney rather than by the petitioner or an entity with legal standing. Under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(iii)(B), the beneficiary of a visa petition is not an 'affected party' entitled to file an appeal, resulting in rejection of the appeal and forfeiture of the filing fee.
